description_long: This fence consists of four vertical wooden planks, each slightly irregular in width and alignment, giving it a rustic appearance. The planks are fastened together by two horizontal metal rails, one near the top and one near the bottom, each secured with prominent, pyramid-shaped metal bolts. The wood is a muted beige color, while the metal rails and bolts are a light gray, providing a subtle contrast. The top edge of the fence is mostly flat but slightly uneven, with one plank slightly taller and another with a small notch at the bottom. The back of the fence is plain, showing only the wood without any visible metal reinforcement. This type of fence would typically be used as a simple barrier in urban parks, gardens, or alleys.
description: A rustic wooden fence with four vertical planks, reinforced by two horizontal metal rails and pyramid-shaped bolts, featuring a slightly uneven top edge.
description_view_0: Front view showing four vertical wooden planks, two horizontal metal rails with pyramid-shaped bolts, and a slightly uneven top edge.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence and the protruding bolts from the metal rails.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying only the plain wooden planks without visible metal rails or bolts.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the thin profile and bolt protrusions.
